summary: Recent updates to the session negotiation component of Firefox focused on improving the handling of Session Description Protocol (SDP) objects, which are crucial for establishing multimedia sessions like voice and video calls. By migrating the `Sdp::Clone()` method to return a `UniquePtr`, Firefox enhances memory management, ensuring more efficient use of resources and reducing potential memory leaks. This change matters because it contributes to a smoother and more reliable user experience during real-time communications, leveraging protocols such as WebRTC — the backbone for modern video conferencing and streaming.
